item.title: Oil Advances as US Blockade of Hormuz Escalates Energy Crisis
item.description: Oil gained at the open as the US moved to blockade the Strait of Hormuz after weekend negotiations between Washington and Tehran failed to yield a deal to end their conflict.

item.title: European Gas Prices Jump After Trump Threatens Hormuz Blockade
item.description: European natural gas prices surged in early Asia trading on Monday, after President Donald Trump said the US will begin a full blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: GFL Said to Near Deal to Buy Secure Waste for Over $4.3 Billion
item.description: GFL Environmental Inc. is closing in on a deal to acquire Canada’s Secure Waste Infrastructure Corp. in a transaction valued at more than C$6 billion ($4.3 billion) including debt, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: A2 Milk Lowers Guidance as Formula Supply Disrupted in China
item.description: New Zealand infant formula supplier a2 Milk lowered its revenue and earnings guidance, citing supply chain disruptions — partly linked to the Iran war — that have slowed shipments to China.

item.title: Forint Jumps to Three-Year High as Hungary’s Orban Is Voted Out
item.description: Hungary’s forint rallied after Prime Minister Viktor Orban conceded defeat in a landslide victory for the pro-European opposition in Sunday’s election.

item.title: Wary Investors Eye Another Escalation in Iran War
item.description: The prospect that the Iran war will reintensify after the failure of peace talks threatens to spark fresh volatility across global markets, after a week that saw a fragile ceasefire drive stocks up and oil down by the most this year.

item.title: Bond Traders Snap Back to Inflation as Higher-for-Longer Sets In
item.description: An increasingly fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran is returning the bond market’s focus to inflation and reinforcing expectations that interest rates will stay higher for longer.

item.title: Benin Begins Vote Count With Ex-Deloitte Executive Poised to Win
item.description: Benin started tallying ballots on Sunday, with Finance Minister Romuald Wadagni widely expected to secure an easy victory in his first bid for the nation’s presidency.

item.title: IMF Chief Says Global Prices Will Take Time to Recede After War
item.description: Global prices will take time to come down to levels seen before the US-Israeli war with Iran even if a ceasefire holds, International Monetary Fund Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva said.

item.title: Hormuz Tracker: Limited Transits Before Trump Announces Blockade
item.description: Shipping flows through the Strait of Hormuz continued to operate at reduced levels on Sunday, before President Donald Trump announced an immediate blockade of the critical maritime chokepoint by the US Navy.

item.title: Apple AI Glasses Will Rival Meta’s With Several Styles, Oval Cameras
item.description: Also: The latest on the foldable iPhone.

item.title: How the US-Iran Talks in Islamabad Ended Without a Deal
item.description: Vice President JD Vance landed in Islamabad on Saturday tasked with ending six weeks of war and overcoming 47 years of enmity — 21 hours later, he flew home without a deal.

item.title: Norway Averts Strike as Unions Clinch Wage Deal With Employers
item.description: Norway’s largest trade unions reached a wage deal with employers for private-sector workers, preventing a strike in western Europe’s top energy exporter.

item.title: Trump Says US Will Blockade Hormuz Strait After Iran Talks Fail
item.description: President Donald Trump said the US would blockade the Strait of Hormuz following the collapse of peace talks with Iran in Islamabad this weekend.

item.title: Earnings Season Kicks Off With War, AI Threat Among Key Worries
item.description: Earnings season is about to start at a crucial time for stocks traders whipsawed by war in the Middle East, concerns around private credit and the disruptive threat of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Oil-Rich Latin America Lures Traders Navigating War Jitters
item.description: Latin American assets have emerged as a haven for emerging-market investors trying to navigate an increasingly volatile global backdrop, buoyed by a roster of oil exporters and greater insulation from tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: Peru Casting Ballots for President, Bicameral Congress in Bid for Stability
item.description: Peruvians are heading to the polls Sunday to elect a new president from among a record 36 candidates and to overhaul the legislature, which for the first time in over three decades will return to a bicameral system.

item.title: The World’s Anti-Recession Guardrails Are Weaker Than Ever
item.description: After years of repeated economic shocks, the world has been left woefully unprepared to deal with the next one.

item.title: Leonard Green Nears $3 Billion Deal for Cumming Group, FT Says
item.description: Private equity firm Leonard Green and Partners is closing in on an acquisition of Cumming Group, a construction project consultancy firm owned by fellow buyout firm New Mountain Capital, the Financial Times reported.

item.title: Event Bets Pose a Problem for Wall Street Firms Looking to Trade
item.description: Prediction markets are booming. Wagers on everything from the return of Jesus Christ to who will win the World Cup have driven weekly volumes on platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi into the billions of dollars.

item.title: Failure of US-Iran Talks Set to Weigh on Risk Assets Monday
item.description: The US and Iran’s failure to strike a peace deal over the weekend is set to weigh on market sentiment and lift demand for safe haven assets on Monday, according to analysts.

item.title: UK’s Reeves to Set Out Plans to Help Business With Energy Bills
item.description: The UK government will set out plans to boost the competitiveness of businesses at a time of rising energy bills, Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves said, while warning against heavy government borrowing.

item.title: India Forex Regulator Criticizes Banks’ Rupee Arbitrage Trades
item.description: A senior Reserve Bank of India official criticized foreign-exchange market makers for their role in aggravating the rupee’s weakness during the Middle East tensions, as the regulator keeps up its tough messaging stance in its defense of the currency.

item.title: Saudi Arabia Says East-West Pipeline Restored to Full Capacity
item.description: Saudi Arabia has restored the full pumping capacity of its East-West pipeline to 7 million barrels a day, rehabilitating a vital link for oil exports via the Red Sea.

item.title: Two Supertankers U-Turn in Hormuz as US-Iran Talks Break Down
item.description: Two empty supertankers attempted to make their way through the Strait of Hormuz and into the Persian Gulf on Sunday, only to make a last-minute U-turn just as peace negotiations between the US and Iran broke down, threatening a fragile ceasefire.

item.title: Mpox in Pakistan’s Sindh Linked to Newborn Deaths, Reports Say
item.description: Health officials in Pakistan’s Sindh province have issued an alert after confirming 14 mpox cases this year, including infections linked to five newborn deaths, according to local media reports.

item.title: Malaysia Faces ‘Critical Period’ For Fuel by June, Minister Says
item.description: Malaysia is implementing measures to address fuel shortages triggered by the conflict in the Middle East, The Star reported, citing Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.

item.title: China Unveils Taiwan Goodwill Steps After Rare Opposition Talks
item.description: China announced a series of policy measures to demonstrate “goodwill” toward Taiwan on Sunday, following a landmark meeting between President Xi Jinping and Taiwan opposition leader Cheng Li-wun.

item.title: Japan to Work With Asian Nations to Ease Oil Bottleneck: Akazawa
item.description: Japan will work closely with Asian countries where essential petroleum-based products including medical equipment are produced, economy minister Ryosei Akazawa said in a Sunday debate program on broadcaster NHK.

item.title: Philippines Asks Facebook to Curb Fake News, Warns of Legal Move
item.description: The Philippine government has asked Meta Platforms Inc., the parent company of Facebook, to implement measures to curb the spread of “false and panic-inducing content” on its platforms, warning of possible legal action if the company fails to act swiftly.
